MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day being continued from January 20, 2026 due to condition of the court calendar after is assigned by Department 206 for Prove-Up Hearing as to Defendant Romulo Regaldo

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: D. Carter;

Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 9:28am and concluding at 9:43am.

Plaintiff Marvin Arnaldo Castanon was present in court with Attorney Stanley Riddell.
Defendant Romulo Regaldo was not present.
Attorney for Defendant Aliu Iscandari was present via zoom.

Plaintiff was assisted by Spanish Interpreter Rosario Del Pilar Espinosa.

Mr. Iscandari communicated to the court both counsel had been communicating on this matter but he has been sick lately and could not respond to Mr. Riddell.

This department sends matter back to department 206 for possible trial setting.

The court was informed by the Staff Attorney in department 501, the February 10 motion for summary judgment in that department will be dismissed and taken off calendar.

This matter is set for February 2, 2026 at 9:30am in dept. 206.

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-20-2026 Assigned To Trial Court

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Jan-20-2026 Continued To Jan-23-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 505

Notice Of Motion And Motion/Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion To Compel Responses From Romulo Regaldo To Special Interrogatories And Form Interrogatories
Off Calendar Jul-10-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF MARVIN CASTANON's Motion To Compel Responses From Romulo Regaldo To Special Interrogatories And Form Interrogatories.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Marvin Castanon's motion to compel defendant Romulo Regalado to respond to Castanon's Special Interrogatories, Set One, and Form Interrogatories, Set One, is granted. Regalado failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (CCP 2030.290(a).) Regalado is ordered to serve verified responses, without objections, within 15 days of entry of this order.

Castanon also seeks sanctions. CCP 2030.290(c) provides that "the court shall impose a monetary sanction . . . against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel a response to interrogatories . . . unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." Sanctions are appropriate on a discovery motion where the losing party does not act with substantial justification in its discovery compliance, even where he does not oppose the motion. (See Rule of Court 3.1348(a).) The court does not find substantial justification and orders Regalado to pay $500 in sanctions to Castanon within 15 days of entry of this order. The court reduces the requested amount on this motion because the Riddell Declaration indicates a total of three hours on all three motions and in any event the court concludes $500 is a reasonable sanction on this motion.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion/Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion To Compel Responses From Romulo Regaldo To Request For Production
Off Calendar Jul-10-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF MARVIN CASTANON's Motion To Compel Responses From Romulo Regaldo To Request For Production.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Marvin Castanon's motion to compel defendant Romulo Regalado to respond to Castanon's Requests for Production of Documents is granted. Regalado failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (CCP 2031.300(a).) Regalado is ordered to serve verified responses, without objections, and produce all responsive documents, within 15 days of entry of this order.

Castanon also seeks sanctions. CCP 2031.300(c) provides that "the court shall impose a monetary sanction . . . against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel a response to interrogatories . . . unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." Sanctions are appropriate on a discovery motion where the losing party does not act with substantial justification in its discovery compliance, even where he does not oppose the motion. (See Rule of Court 3.1348(a).) The court does not find substantial justification and orders Regalado to pay $500 in sanctions to Castanon within 15 days of entry of this order. The court reduces the requested amount on this motion because the Riddell Declaration indicates a total of three hours on all three motions and in any event the court concludes $500 is a reasonable sanction on this motion.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion/Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion To Have Requesets For Admissions Deemed Admitted
Off Calendar Jul-10-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF MARVIN CASTANON's Motion To Have Requests For Admissions Deemed Admitted.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Marvin Castanon's motion to deem admitted the truth of any matters specified in his Requests for Admission, Set One, served on defendant Romulo Regalado, is granted. Regalado failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (CCP 2033.280(a).) The truth of any matters specified in the RFAs is deemed admitted. (CCP 2033.280(b), (c).)

Castanon also seeks sanctions. CCP 2033.280(c) further provides: "It is mandatory that the court impose a monetary sanction under Chapter 7 (commencing with Section 2023.010) on the party or attorney, or both, whose failure to serve a timely response to requests for admission necessitated this motion." The court awards sanctions of $500 payable by Regalado to Castanon within 15 days of entry of this order. The court reduces the requested amount on this motion because the Riddell Declaration indicates a total of three hours on all three motions and in any event the court concludes $500 is a reasonable sanction on this motion.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
